title: Gladiolus huttonii
taxonomy: Gladiolus huttonii is a species of flowering plant in the family Iridaceae. It belongs to the genus Gladiolus, which is a group of plants commonly known as gladiolus or sword lilies. Gladiolus huttonii is native to Australia, specifically in the eastern regions. The classification of Gladiolus huttonii places it within the order Asparagales and the subclass Liliidae. This species is further classified into several subspecies based on its physical characteristics and geographical distribution.

intro: Gladiolus huttonii, commonly known as the Himalayan gladiolus, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Iridaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, which may help alleviate symptoms of arthritis, reduce oxidative stress, and prevent infections. The bioactive constituents of Gladiolus huttonii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to make herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various ailments including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues.
